commit.message:
feat: add OpenAI provider support (BAT-315) (#232)

* feat: add OpenAI as second AI provider with scalable registry architecture (BAT-315)

Node.js:
- Provider registry pattern (providers/index.js) — register/getAdapter with fallback
- Claude adapter (providers/claude.js) — extracted from claude.js, full interface
- OpenAI adapter (providers/openai.js) — Chat Completions API, streaming, tool use
- Neutral internal message format — both adapters translate to/from it
- claude.js refactored to provider-agnostic orchestrator
- OpenAI SSE streaming support in web.js (incremental tool arg accumulation)
- OpenAI key redaction in security.js
- config.js: PROVIDER + OPENAI_KEY support

Android:
- Providers.kt — ProviderInfo registry, OpenAI model list, modelsForProvider()
- ConfigManager.kt — encrypted OpenAI key storage, provider field, activeCredential
- ProviderConfigScreen.kt — tabbed UI (Claude | OpenAI) with connection test
- NavGraph.kt — ProviderConfigRoute + backward-compat AnthropicConfigRoute redirect
- SettingsScreen.kt — "AI Provider" field showing provider + model
- SettingsHelpTexts.kt — PROVIDER and OPENAI_API_KEY help texts
- Models.kt — cross-provider modelDisplayName lookup

Migration-safe: all new fields default to Claude behavior for existing users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 5 Copilot review comments (BAT-315)

- writeConfigJson: write real Anthropic credential, not activeCredential
  (was duplicating OpenAI key into anthropicApiKey field)
- OpenAI rate limit: convert duration string ("6s") to ISO timestamp
  (was causing NaN in rate-limit pre-check Date parsing)
- SettingsScreen: use providerById().displayName instead of replaceFirstChar
  (was showing "Openai" instead of "OpenAI")
- Connection test: use Claude-specific credential per tab, not activeCredential
  (was passing OpenAI key to Anthropic test when OpenAI was active)
- Model picker: auto-activate tab's provider when saving model
  (prevents provider/model mismatch at runtime)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 3 Copilot R2 review comments (BAT-315)

- Split activeCredential (Anthropic-only) from activeProviderKey (provider-aware)
  to prevent SetupScreen/legacy flows from using OpenAI key as Anthropic credential.
  Updated all provider-aware call sites (ConfigClaimImporter, runtimeValidation,
  DashboardScreen, SettingsScreen) to use activeProviderKey.
- Deduplicate testAnthropicConnection: make ProviderConfigScreen's version
  internal, remove duplicate from AnthropicConfigScreen (same package resolves)
- SettingsHelpTexts.MODEL: word as examples rather than exhaustive list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 4 Copilot R3 review comments (BAT-315)

- Fix activeProviderKey KDoc to reflect actual usage (validation, UI)
- Add IOException/SocketTimeoutException catch to testAnthropicConnection
- Widen sk-*** redaction pattern to include _ and - characters
- Gate Claude usage polling on PROVIDER === 'claude'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot R4 cleanup comments (BAT-315)

- Align maskKey() pattern with maskSensitive() (6 leading chars, not 8)
- Remove unused Arrangement import from ProviderConfigScreen
- Remove unused AUTH_TYPE import from providers/claude.js

Note: shared composables coupling (AnthropicConfigScreen → ProviderConfigScreen)
acknowledged as future refactor opportunity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 2 Copilot R5 comments (BAT-315)

- config.js PROVIDER: add type guard for non-string/null values
- Model picker: initialize selection from current provider's model list,
  preventing save of cross-provider model mismatch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 4 Copilot R6 comments (BAT-315)

- Add MAX_HISTORY trim after tool loop (prevents unbounded growth)
- Fix stale comments referencing old activeCredential behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 4 Copilot R7 comments (BAT-315)

- Guard models[0] with firstOrNull() in provider switch + model picker
- Add IOException/SocketTimeoutException catch to testOpenAIConnection
- MAX_HISTORY trim: remove assistant+tool groups atomically to prevent
  orphaned tool calls/results that would cause API rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 3 Copilot R8 comments (BAT-315)

- Clamp selectedTab to known provider via providerById()
- Validate PROVIDER against supported set, fallback to 'claude'
- Make authLabel provider-aware (force 'api-key' for OpenAI)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing providerById import (BAT-315)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace segmented tabs with provider rows, update OpenAI models (BAT-315)

UI audit fixes:
- Replace foreign SegmentedButton tabs with two full-width provider rows
  matching existing ConfigField/ProviderConfigField pattern
- Active provider shows "Active" badge in Accent color
- Tapping inactive provider switches immediately with restart toast
- Show only active provider's fields (no more tab concept)
- Model picker always shows active provider's models (no cross-provider mismatch)

OpenAI models updated:
- Added o3 (reasoning), o3-mini (fast reasoning)
- Keep gpt-4.1 family + o4-mini

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update OpenAI models to latest — GPT-5.2, Codex Max, o4-mini, o3 (BAT-315)

Remove outdated GPT-4.1 family. Current model list:
- gpt-5.2 (flagship)
- gpt-5.1-codex-max (agentic coding)
- o4-mini (reasoning)
- o3 (deep reasoning)

Also update default model fallback in config.js.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: trim OpenAI models to latest only — GPT-5.2, GPT-5.3 Codex (BAT-315)

Remove gpt-5-mini and gpt-5-nano; keep only flagship (5.2),
agentic coding (5.3 Codex), and reasoning (o4-mini, o3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove reasoning models from OpenAI list — only GPT-5.2 + 5.3 Codex (BAT-315)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace gpt-5.3-codex with gpt-5.2-codex — 5.3 is OAuth-only (BAT-315)

gpt-5.3-codex returns 404 with standard API keys (only available
via ChatGPT OAuth endpoint). gpt-5.2-codex works on standard API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: codex models need max_completion_tokens not max_tokens (BAT-315)

gpt-5.2-codex returns 400 with max_tokens param. Add /codex/i
check alongside reasoning model regex so both use
max_completion_tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use max_completion_tokens for all OpenAI models (BAT-315)

All current OpenAI models (GPT-5.x, o-series, codex) require
max_completion_tokens — max_tokens is deprecated. Simplify by
always using max_completion_tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove gpt-5.2-codex — requires /v1/responses endpoint (BAT-315)

Codex models use the Responses API, not Chat Completions.
Keep only gpt-5.2 which works with our chat/completions adapter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: migrate OpenAI adapter to Responses API + add Codex model (BAT-315)

Route all OpenAI models through /v1/responses instead of /v1/chat/completions.
The Responses API is OpenAI's future — supports Codex models natively and
will eventually replace Chat Completions for all models.

Changes:
- openai.js: rewrite message translation for Responses API input format
  (function_call/function_call_output items, input_image for vision,
  instructions field for system prompt, max_output_tokens)
- web.js: replace SSE parser with typed event handler
  (response.output_text.delta, response.function_call_arguments.delta,
  response.completed, response.incomplete, fallback from accumulated deltas)
- claude.js: accept 'openai-responses' stream protocol
- Providers.kt: add codex-mini-latest model

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 3 Copilot review comments (BAT-315)

- Remove unused imports in AnthropicConfigScreen.kt (Dispatchers,
  withContext, JSONObject, HttpURLConnection, URL)
- Update provider help text: "GPT-4.1 family" → "GPT-5.2, Codex Mini"
- Parse OpenAI error body in testOpenAIConnection for actionable messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: task-store checkpoint handles neutral message format (BAT-315)

Orphan stripping and secret redaction now handle both Claude-native
(tool_use/tool_result blocks) and neutral format (role:'tool' messages,
assistant.toolCalls arrays) used by the OpenAI adapter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: preserve OpenAI key/provider when re-running setup (BAT-315)

SetupScreen.saveAndStart() now loads existing config to preserve
openaiApiKey and provider fields, preventing "Run Setup Again" from
wiping previously saved OpenAI credentials.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: config import preserves OpenAI fields, sanitizer handles both formats, codex-5.3 (BAT-315)

- ConfigClaimImporter now parses provider + openaiApiKey from claim JSON
- sanitizeConversation handles both Claude-native (tool_use/tool_result
  blocks) and neutral format (toolCalls/role:'tool') for legacy checkpoints
- Codex model changed from codex-mini-latest to codex-5.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct Codex model ID to gpt-5.3-codex (BAT-315)

codex-mini-latest is deprecated. The correct API model ID is
gpt-5.3-codex per OpenAI's models page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle legacy checkpoint format + provider-aware model default (BAT-315)

- claude.js toApiMessages: pass through Claude-native content arrays
  from legacy checkpoints instead of wrapping in invalid text block
- ConfigClaimImporter: parse provider before model, default to
  provider-appropriate model (gpt-5.2 for openai, claude-opus-4-6 for claude)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: flatten tool schema for Responses API (BAT-315)

Responses API expects {type, name, description, parameters} at top level,
NOT nested under {function: {...}} like Chat Completions.
Fixes "Missing required parameter: tools[0].name" error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: strengthen skill/tool instructions for OpenAI models (BAT-315)

Codex/GPT models don't follow tool-use directives in system prompts
as reliably as Claude — they describe tools instead of calling them.

Three changes:
1. Skills section: OpenAI gets direct "follow injected instructions"
   vs Claude's "use skill_read to load" indirection
2. New "CRITICAL: Tool Use Behavior" section for OpenAI emphasizing
   tools must be called, not described
3. Active Skills dynamic section uses MANDATORY language for OpenAI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert: remove provider-specific prompt changes, keep universal (BAT-315)

OpenClaw uses identical system prompts for all providers — no branching.
The skill/tool issue on Codex is a model capability limitation, not
something we should work around with prompt divergence.

Reverts the 3 provider-specific sections added in 4c6e049.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* debug: add BAT-315 logging for OpenAI tool call diagnosis

Codex responds with text but never calls tools. Adding logs to see:
- Tool count sent to API
- Response output types and tool call count
- Whether parsing is losing function_call items

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add strict:false to OpenAI tool schemas — enables tool calling (BAT-315)

Responses API defaults to strict:true for function tools, which requires
additionalProperties:false on every object in the parameters schema.
Our tools don't have that, so the API silently ignores them — the model
never sees tools and responds with text only.

Adding strict:false opts out of strict validation, matching Chat
Completions behavior. This is the root cause of Codex never calling tools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* debug: log sample tool JSON to verify schema format (BAT-315)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add reasoning parameter for Codex models (BAT-315)

Codex (gpt-5.3-codex) is a reasoning model like o-series — it needs
the reasoning.effort parameter to function properly. Without it,
tool calling degrades and the model responds with text only.

OpenClaw/pi-ai always passes a reasoning level for Codex models.
Setting effort:'low' + summary:'auto' as baseline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add GPT-5.2 Pro and GPT-5 Mini to model list (BAT-315)

- Add gpt-5.2-pro (smartest, expensive) and gpt-5-mini (fast, cheap)
- Add reasoning param for Pro models (required like Codex)
- Use generic text in help tooltips (no specific model names)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: trim OpenAI models to GPT-5.2 + Codex only (BAT-315)

Remove Pro and Mini — Pro is 12x more expensive than GPT-5.2,
Mini is too weak for a 56-tool agent. Keep it simple: one
general-purpose model, one code agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove BAT-315 debug logging from claude.js

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle Claude-native message blocks in OpenAI adapter (BAT-315)

Translate legacy assistant content[tool_use] and user content[tool_result]
blocks into Responses API function_call/function_call_output items.
Prevents broken resume flows when switching providers mid-conversation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: force provider to claude in Setup flow (BAT-315)

Setup only validates Claude credentials, so re-running setup while on
OpenAI provider could create inconsistent config. Force provider back
to claude — OpenAI is configured separately in Settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: normalize provider field on config import (BAT-315)

Trim/lowercase provider string and validate against allowlist to prevent
mismatched provider IDs from breaking runtime selection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add token-based auth for cross-origin requests

- Store token in localStorage on frontend
- Send token via Authorization header
- API accepts both cookie and Authorization header
- Fixes auth in incognito/third-party cookie blocking scenarios

commit.message:
feat(steps): Timeline Step Editor redesign + ActiveBrew timeline (#32)

* feat(steps): Timeline Step Editor redesign + ActiveBrew timeline

Replace the confusing step input UI (raw seconds, cryptic labels) with a
timeline-based editor using human-readable time ranges and compact/expand
cards.

Phase 1 — StepEditor redesign:
- Smart time input: type seconds, displays MM:SS, commits on blur
- Collapsed one-liners: "0:00 → 0:40 · Bloom · pour to 42g"
- Accordion expand: tap to edit name, time range, water, note
- Split-step: divide a step 50/50 in planning context
- Vertical timeline bar connecting step dots
- Diff annotations via colored left borders on collapsed steps

Phase 2 — ActiveBrew timeline:
- Past steps: green dot + compact one-liner with tapped time
- Current step: pulsing dot + expanded card with mini progress bar
- Future steps: empty dot + dimmed one-liner
- Recipe reference strip: "18g · 5-2 · 270g target" pinned in header

No data model changes. Step format stays { id, name, time, duration,
waterTo, note }. All existing props preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings from timeline step editor

- Extract stepTimeRange() helper to deduplicate formula in StepOneLiner
  and RemovedStepOneLiner
- Fix handleSplit name-suffix accumulation bug: strip existing "(N)"
  suffix before re-numbering (prevents "Bloom (1) (1)")
- Guard handleSplit Math.max against empty arrays
- Fix isPast incorrectly classifying skipped steps as past in
  ActiveBrew — skipped steps now render exclusively via skipped branch
- Collapse expanded step when disabled prop flips to true
- Simplify recipe reference strip with filter-join pattern
- Add clarifying comment on recascade mutation contract

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: compound 2 solutions from timeline step editor review

- split-operation-accumulates-name-suffixes: string append on re-split
  produces unbounded name growth; strip existing suffix first
- boolean-state-classifications-must-be-mutually-exclusive: overlapping
  isPast/skipped flags cause wrong step classification in ActiveBrew

Updates CLAUDE.md solution count to 29 (13 logic-errors).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Social Post + API Connection nodes, OAuth infrastructure, Trigger node rebrand

- Add Social Post node (social-post) with platform-specific content generation,
  expanded view with platform toggles, intent/tone config, and preview tabs
- Add API Connection node (api-connection) with OAuth connect/disconnect,
  platform posting engine, and post history log
- Add OAuth infrastructure: platform credentials table, token management,
  authorize/callback routes for X, LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Reddit
- Add Platform Integrations dialog for managing social account connections
- Rebrand Timer Event node as Trigger with two modes: Timed (interval pulses)
  and Automated (fires when upstream node completes)
- Add social_post_logs table for posting history tracking
- Add encrypted credential storage with auto-refresh token lifecycle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Harden security and open up page spacing

Security: fix SQL injection in hasColumn via table whitelist, sanitize
X-Forwarded-For before storing, add per-IP rate limiting to auth routes,
validate language cookie value at runtime, patch npm vulnerabilities.

Design: increase padding, gaps, and margins throughout to reduce visual
density across the home page, catalog panels, and profile builds browser.

commit.message:
Fix Zoom recordings group filtering not blocking restricted classes

- Fix isAdmin check: use isAdminAuthenticated() instead of currentUser.role
  (currentUser never had a 'role' property, so isAdmin was always false)
- Fix user email lookup: also check currentUser.email as fallback
- Fix filter logic: students with no group now see ONLY "Todos" recordings
  (was using redundant check that still returned true for unassigned recordings)
- Apply same fixes to renderZoomRecordings, updateZoomCardCounts, openZoomRec
- Add console log for debugging group filter issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix sprites: remove cross-section dependency from sprite loading

spritesLoaded required both side + cross images to succeed.
Since we switched to side-view only, cross-section failure
blocked all sprites from rendering.

commit.message:
fix: correct boundary assertion in ROI cap test

The test asserted > 100 but ROI is capped at exactly 100.0. Changed to >= 100.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add inpaint canvas features: soft brush, shape tools, canvas expansion

- Soft brush with hardness slider (0-100): radial gradient falloff for
  feathered mask edges, works for both brush and erase modes
- Stroke interpolation: fills gaps when painting fast (step = size/4)
- Shape fill tools: Rect and Ellipse with hard/soft edges based on hardness
- Shape preview canvas shows semi-transparent outline while dragging
- Canvas expansion: expand image borders for outpainting, pre-fills mask
  in expansion zones, updates referenceImageBase64 for API
- Fix inpaint bug fixes: mini canvas positioning, fullscreen sizing,
  changes preserved on fullscreen close
- All features work in both mini and fullscreen modal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix dropdown not opening — stop click propagation from toggle buttons

commit.message:
Practice Tutorial tab — scenario selector, 4 Wingspan tutorials (A-D), CTA congrats flow [checklist passed]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor into modular architecture and add desktop packaging

- Split monolithic whisper2text.py into audio/, config/, engine/, ui/ modules
- Switch backend from OpenAI whisper to pywhispercpp (whisper.cpp with CUDA)
- Add centralized path resolution (config/paths.py) with PyInstaller support
- Add process lock, structured logging, error panel, settings dialog
- Add evdev-based global hotkey listener (Wayland-compatible)
- Add model manager with HuggingFace download support
- Create PyInstaller spec with CUDA runtime hook
- Add install.sh/uninstall.sh for systemd service deployment

fix: switch base image from Alpine to node:22-slim (Debian)

node-llama-cpp requires glibc to use prebuilt binaries.
Alpine (musl libc) forces a full llama.cpp source build needing
cmake/make/g++ and still fails. node:22-slim resolves this cleanly.

git remains only in the installer stage; the runtime image stays clean.

security: auditoría completa + card-selector UX en settings

Seguridad:
- Webhook HMAC: timing-safe comparison con crypto.timingSafeEqual (WhatsApp, Instagram, Wompi)
- Auth guards en server actions sin proteger (ensureTablesExist, getLandingPlans, etc.)
- Rate limiting en endpoints públicos (webchat, webhooks, registro, formulario contacto)
- CSP header + HSTS preload + allowedOrigins dinámico desde env
- CORS restrictivo en webchat para producción (no más wildcard *)
- Sanitización de error.message: nunca exponer detalles internos al cliente
- .env.example: placeholders seguros (no tokens por defecto)
- npm overrides: hono ^4.12.4 y @hono/node-server ^1.19.10

- Card-selector pattern en tabs Canales, IA y Créditos, Facturación

commit.message:
Add MCP tools for stacking lottery (stackspot) skill (#258)

* feat: add MCP tools for stackspot stacking lottery skill (#210)

Adds `stacking-lottery.tools.ts` with 6 MCP tools corresponding to each
stackspot skill subcommand:
- stackspot_list_pots: list known pots with on-chain value and lock status
- stackspot_get_pot_state: fetch full on-chain state (value, configs, pool config, details)
- stackspot_join_pot: contribute STX to a pot (with minimum-amount validation)
- stackspot_start_pot: trigger stacking via the platform contract during PoX prepare phase
- stackspot_claim_rewards: claim sBTC yield / recover STX after stacking completes
- stackspot_cancel_pot: cancel an unlocked pot to recover STX before stacking

Contract constants (deployer, platform address, known pots) are mirrored from
the stackspot skill source. All tools are mainnet-only, matching the skill.
Registers under Layer 2b (DeFi) in the tool registry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fix claim-rewards: remove incorrect contractPrincipalCV arg (takes 0 args)
- Fix cancel-pot: same fix (takes 0 args)

- Button grayed out when no API key configured; toggles translation on/off

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Prometheus alerting rules + Alertmanager (P3 observability complete)

- monitoring/alerts.yml — 6 alert rules (block rate, risk score, trifecta, volume, missing)
- monitoring/alertmanager.yml — severity routing; Slack/PagerDuty/email templates (commented)
- monitoring/docker-compose.yml — adds Alertmanager service (port 9093)
- monitoring/prometheus.yml — wires rule_files + alerting config
- monitoring/grafana/provisioning/datasources/prometheus.yml — adds Alertmanager datasource
- monitoring/README.md — alerting section with table of all 6 rules

All 4 services verified healthy. 6 alert rules loaded and evaluated by Prometheus.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: chown vault/data volumes on startup for permission compatibility

Add entrypoint script that runs as root to fix ownership of mounted
volumes before dropping to the nextjs user via su-exec. Handles
files created by the obsidian-sync sidecar or previous root runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: merge consecutive system messages for OpenAI providers (#710)

* fix: merge consecutive system messages for OpenAI providers

OpenAI reasoning models (e.g., gpt-5-nano) don't handle multiple
consecutive system/developer messages well, producing empty responses.
This adds a provider-specific mergeConsecutiveSystemMessages transform
in LLMExecutor, following the existing filterReasoningPartsForProvider
pattern. Only applies to openai and azure-openai providers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix formatting

repository.description: Perstack: A harness for micro-agents. Define experts and execute them at scale.

commit.message:
Add Denver County adapter and fix prompt county references

Denver adapter uses ArcGIS Online (PROP_PARCELS_A for parcels/values,
residential_characteristics for building attrs, sales_and_transfers
for full sales history). Full building data support.

Also fix prompt builder to use the selected county name instead of
hardcoded "Boulder County" in the system instruction and rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: enable native keyring backends for credential persistence

Enable platform-specific keyring features to ensure API keys persist
across application restarts:
- windows-native: Windows Credential Manager
- apple-native: macOS Keychain
- linux-native: Secret Service / kernel keyutils

Without these features, keyring uses an in-memory backend that loses
credentials when the process exits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Side-view sprites everywhere, fix scorecard save, remove scissors

- Switch all cross-section sprite usage to side-view (scorecard, showcase, share card)
- Add drawSideSprite() helper with 2.5:1 aspect ratio and procedural fallback
- Add crossOrigin to sprite loader to prevent canvas tainting on share card
- Shift scorecard thumbnails left for more spacing from bars
- Remove scissors flanking average score on results screen
